curriculum.subject: Chemistry
curriculum.overallDescription: 9 units, 9 chapters, and 11 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Understand nature of matter, properties, measurement, uncertainty, laws of combination, atomic theory
Calculate mole concept, molar masses, percentage composition, stoichiometry
Represent chemical phenomena at macroscopic, molecular, and symbolic levels
Trace discoveries of sub-atomic particles and atomic models
Explain developments leading to Bohr's Model for Hydrogen Atom
Describe the Quantum Mechanical Model of the Atom
Explain the need for classification and genesis of periodic classification
Apply Modern Periodic Law and nomenclature rules (Z > 100)
Correlate electronic configuration with position in table and element type (s, p, d, f blocks)
Apply Kossel-Lewis approach for Ionic/Electrovalent bonds
Analyze bond parameters and hybridization using Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion (VSEPR)

Comparing the pH of strong and weak acids of same concentration

practicals.details: Evaluation Scheme Component. Includes preparation of standard solutions and titration.

practicals.title: Salt Analysis (08 Marks)
practicals.details: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt from prescribed list. Includes detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds.

practicals.title: Content Based Experiment (06 Marks)
Study shift in equilibrium between ferric ions and thiocyanate ions
practicals.details: Chemical Equilibrium. Study effect of concentration changes.

practicals.title: Study shift in equilibrium between [Co(H2O)6]2+ and chloride ions
practicals.details: Chemical Equilibrium. Study effect of concentration changes.

Preparation of standard solution of Sodium carbonate and determination of HCl strength
Study the presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
practicals.details: Class XII Project. Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing.

practicals.title: Study quantity of casein present in milk samples
Preparation of soybean milk and comparison with natural milk regarding curd formation/temperature effect
Study the effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions
Study digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH/temperature
Comparative study of rate of fermentation (wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice)
Extraction of essential oils from Saunf, Ajwain, Illaichi
Study common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric powder, chili powder and pepper

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

internal_assessment.scheme_name: Practical Examination for Visually Challenged Learners (Classes XI and XII)
components: Written test (based on given/prescribed practical's): 10 Marks
requirements: Written examination conducted at the time of practical examination.
Question paper contains total of 15 skill-based very short answer questions; student answers any 10.
Student required to maintain a practical file recording at least five listed experiments.
Practicals checked and signed by internal examiner.

experiments: Cutting glass tube and glass rod
Bending a glass tube
Drawing out a glass jet
Boring a cork

experiment_sections.section_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
experiments: Determination of melting point of an organic compound.
Determination of boiling point of an organic compound.
Crystallization of impure sample (Alum, Copper Sulphate, Benzoic Acid).
Determination of pH of solutions from fruit juices/known concentrations.
Comparing the pH of strong and weak acids.
Study pH change in titration using universal indicator.
Study pH change by common-ion effect.
Shift in equilibrium between ferric ions and thiocyanate ions.
Shift in equilibrium between [Co(H2O)6]2+ and chloride ions.
Using mechanical/electronic balance.
Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid.
Determination of strength of NaOH by titration against Oxalic acid.
Preparation of standard solution of Sodium carbonate.
Determination of strength of HCl by titration against Sodium Carbonate.
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (specific list provided).
Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds.
Crystallization (Copper sulphate or Benzoic acid)
Determination of pH using pH paper.
Comparing pH of strong and weak acids.
Shift in equilibrium (Ferric ions/thiocyanate).
Shift in equilibrium ([Co(H2O)6]2+/chloride).
Preparation of standard solution of oxalic acid.
Determination of molarity of NaOH.
Determination of one anion and one cation (specific list provided).
Detection of Nitrogen.
Detection of Halogen.

practical_assessment.assessment_notes: Micro-chemical methods should be used wherever possible. Topics like s & p Block Elements and Gaseous State are assessed formatively only.

experiments: Preparation of lyophilic sol (starch, egg albumin, gum).
Preparation of lyophobic sol (aluminum hydroxide, ferric hydroxide, arsenous sulphide).
Dialysis of sols.
Study role of emulsifying agents.
Effect of concentration and temperature on rate (Sodium Thiosulphate + HCl).
Reaction rates: Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide.
Clock reaction: Potassium Iodate, Sodium Sulphate.
Enthalpy of dissolution (Copper Sulphate or KNO3).
Enthalpy of neutralization (HCl + NaOH).
Determination of enthalpy change (Acetone and Chloroform).
Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu with concentration changes.
Separation of pigments (leaves/flowers) and Rf determination.
Separation of constituents in inorganic mixture.
Double salt: Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum.
Potassium Ferric Oxalate.
Acetanilide
Di-benzal acetone
p-Nitroacetanilide
Aniline yellow/2-Naphthol Aniline dye
Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic, amino groups.
Tests for carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples/foodstuffs.
Determination of concentration/molarity against Oxalic acid.
Determination of concentration/molarity against Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate.
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (specific list provided).
Preparation of lyophilic sol.
Preparation of lyophobic sol.
Separation of pigments and Rf determination.
Alcoholic/Carboxylic groups.
Aldehyde/Ketonic groups.
Tests in given foodstuffs.
Potash Alum
(a) Preparation of standard Oxalic acid solution.
(b) Determination of molarity of KMnO4 against Oxalic acid.
Determination of one anion and one cation (specific list provided).

practical_assessment.assessment_notes: Micro-chemical methods should be used wherever possible. Topics like Surface Chemistry, Isolation of Elements, Polymers, and Everyday Life are assessed formatively only.

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_projects: Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion.
Study of methods of purification of water.
Testing hardness/presence of Iron/Fluoride/Chloride in drinking water.
Investigation of foaming capacity of washing soaps and effect of Sodium carbonate.
Study acidity of different samples of tea leaves.
Determination of rate of evaporation of different liquids.
Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers.
Study acidity of fruit and vegetable juices.

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources. Any investigatory project involving about 10 periods can be chosen with teacher approval.
suggested_projects: Study of presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening.
Study of quantity of casein present in milk samples.
Preparation of soybean milk and comparison with natural milk (curd formation, temperature effect).
Effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions.
Digestion of starch by salivary amylase; effect of pH and temperature.
Comparative study of fermentation rate: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice.
Extraction of essential oils (Saunf, Ajwain, Illaichi).
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric powder, chili powder, pepper.

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_projects: Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion.
Study of methods of purification of water.
Testing hardness/presence of Iron/Fluoride/Chloride in drinking water.
Investigation of foaming capacity of washing soaps and effect of Sodium carbonate.
Study acidity of different samples of tea leaves.
Determination of rate of evaporation of different liquids.
Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers.

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources. Any investigatory project involving about 10 periods can be chosen with teacher approval.
suggested_projects: Study of presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening.
Study of quantity of casein present in milk samples.
Preparation of soybean milk and comparison with natural milk (curd formation, temperature effect).
Effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions.
Digestion of starch by salivary amylase; effect of pH and temperature.
Comparative study of fermentation rate: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice.
Extraction of essential oils (Saunf, Ajwain, Illaichi).
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric powder, chili powder, pepper.

long_chapter_names_reclassified: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Conceptual Foundations and Measurement
competency_groups.description: Equip learners with tools to understand the working of Chemistry, develop conceptual foundations, represent phenomena at macroscopic/molecular/symbolic levels, identify patterns, and apply knowledge to real-life situations.
competencies: Understand nature of matter, properties, measurement, uncertainty, laws of combination, atomic theory
Calculate mole concept, molar masses, percentage composition, stoichiometry
Represent chemical phenomena at macroscopic, molecular, and symbolic levels

competency_groups.title: Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to comprehend the evolution from sub-atomic particles discovery through Bohr's model to the quantum mechanical model.
competencies: Trace discoveries of sub-atomic particles and atomic models
Explain developments leading to Bohr's Model for Hydrogen Atom
Describe the Quantum Mechanical Model of the Atom

competency_groups.title: Periodicity and Classification
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify elements, understand periodic laws, electronic configurations, block types, and trends in properties.
competencies: Explain the need for classification and genesis of periodic classification
Apply Modern Periodic Law and nomenclature rules (Z > 100)
Correlate electronic configuration with position in table and element type (s, p, d, f blocks)
Analyze periodic trends in properties
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding Theories
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to apply various bonding theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital) and understand bond parameters.
competencies: Apply Kossel-Lewis approach for Ionic/Electrovalent bonds
Analyze bond parameters and hybridization using Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion (VSEPR)
Explain bonding via Valence Bond Theory and Molecular Orbital Theory
Describe Hydrogen bonding in homonuclear diatomic molecules

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics and Spontaneity
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to measure thermodynamic terms, enthalpy changes, and determine spontaneity using Gibbs Energy.
competencies: Measure ∆U and ∆H via calorimetry
Calculate Enthalpy Change for different reaction types
Determine spontaneity of reactions using Gibbs Energy change

competency_groups.title: Equilibrium Principles
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to analyze dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes, including factors affecting it.
competencies: Define Dynamic Equilibrium and Law of Chemical Equilibrium
Distinguish between Homogeneous and Heterogeneous equilibria
Relate K, Q, and Gibbs Energy (G)
Analyze factors affecting equilibrium in ionic solutions, acids/bases, buffers, and solubility

competency_groups.title: Redox Processes
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify oxidation-reduction reactions through classical ideas, electron transfer, and oxidation numbers.
competencies: Classify Redox Reactions based on Oxidation/Reduction concepts
Analyze redox processes in terms of electron transfer
Assign and calculate Oxidation Numbers

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Fundamentals
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand carbon tetravalence, structural representations, nomenclature, isomerism, and reaction mechanisms.
competencies: Represent organic compounds structurally based on shapes of bonds
Apply IUPAC nomenclature rules for classification
Identify types of Isomerism
Analyze fundamental concepts in Organic Reaction Mechanisms
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.chapter_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbon Classification and Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify hydrocarbons, understand their properties, and assess toxicity.
competencies: Classify Alkanes, Alkenes, Alkynes, and Aromatic Hydrocarbons
Analyze carcinogenic nature and toxicity of specific compounds

competency_groups.title: Solutions and Colligative Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to express concentration, understand solubility principles, and apply colligative properties for molar mass determination.
competencies: Differentiate types of solutions (Ideal/Non-Ideal)
Apply expressions for vapor pressure and colligative properties
Determine Molar Mass using abnormal molecular masses

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry and Energy Conversion
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand electrochemical cells, Nernst equation applications, and corrosion mechanisms.
competencies: Explain Galvanic Cells and Electrolytic Cells principles
Apply Nernst Equation for cell potential calculations
Analyze conductance in electrolytic solutions

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics and Reaction Rates
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to analyze factors influencing reaction rates, integrated rate equations, and collision theory.
competencies: Calculate Rate of Chemical reactions using integrated rate equations
Analyze temperature dependence of reaction rates

competency_groups.title: d- and f-Block Elements Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand electronic configurations, general properties of transition elements, lanthanoids/actinoids.
competencies: Correlate position in periodic table with d/f-block configuration
Describe general properties of Transition Elements and important compounds

competency_groups.title: Coordination Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to apply Werner's theory, nomenclature rules, and understand bonding in coordination compounds.
competencies: Apply Werner's Theory of Coordination Compounds
Use IUPAC nomenclature for complex ions
Analyze Isomerism types

competency_groups.title: Organic Halogen Derivatives and Functional Groups
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify haloalkanes/haloarenes, understand preparation methods, and analyze alcohol/phenol/ether properties.
competencies: Classify Haloalkanes/Haloarenes based on C-X bond nature

competency_groups.title: Carbonyl Compounds Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand nomenclature, preparation methods, physical properties, and chemical reactions of aldehydes/ketones/carboxylic acids.
competencies: Differentiate Carbonyl Group structures

competency_groups.title: Amines Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify amines, understand preparation methods including Diazonium salts.
competencies: Classify Amines and apply nomenclature rules

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules Structure and Function
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify structures of carbohydrates, proteins, enzymes, vitamins, nucleic acids, and hormones.
competencies: Classify Biomolecules (Carbohydrates, Proteins, Enzymes)

competency_groups.title: Surface Chemistry and Colloids
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to distinguish between true solutions, colloids, suspensions, and understand adsorption/emulsions.
competencies: Distinguish lyophilic/lyophobic properties of colloids

competency_groups.title: Chemistry in Everyday Life and Industry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand applications of chemicals in medicines, food preservation, isolation principles, and polymers.
competencies: Identify uses of analgesics, antiseptics, antibiotics

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

internal_assessment.scheme_name: Practical Examination for Visually Challenged Learners (Classes XI and XII)
components: Written test (based on given/prescribed practical's): 10 Marks
requirements: Written examination conducted at the time of practical examination.
Question paper contains total of 15 skill-based very short answer questions; student answers any 10.
Student required to maintain a practical file recording at least five listed experiments.
Practicals checked and signed by internal examiner.
